en: All right, great. Okay, I just gotta go pick up my books. I'll be right back. Don't go anywhere.
ko: 좋아, 좋아. 나 책 가지러 갔다 올게. 금방 올게. 어디 가지 마.

en: No problem. I'll be right here.
Nice to see you again, Maria. Hello, Mrs. Lafferty.
ko: 또 만나서 반가워요, 마리아. 안녕하세요, 래퍼티 선생님.

en: Oh, welcome. What is your name? Uh, I'm Joey.
ko: 오, 환영해요. 이름이 뭐예요? 어, 저는 조이예요.

en: Joey, you have a wonderful accent.
Thank you.
Joey, this is an English as a Second Language class for beginners.
ko: 조이, 이건 초급자를 위한 제2외국어 영어 수업이에요.

en: Are you sure you're in the right place?
Oh, I'm in the right place. Let's get started.
I hope that everybody practiced counting to ten over the weekend.
ko: 주말 동안 다들 10까지 세는 연습 했길 바라요.

en: Great. Let's do it together.
Very good, boys. Somebody's gonna get a gold star.
School, Renata. They go to school.
Okay, Joey, why don't you pick it up where Renata left off?
ko: 좋아요, 조이, 레나타가 멈춘 데서 이어할래요?

en: Now, this is a hard part, so take your time.
ko: 자, 이 부분은 어려우니까 천천히 하세요.

en: Oh, boy. Yeah, this looks tough.
The bell rang and the students all went to the auditorium with their teacher. Boom!
ko: 종이 울리고 학생들이 모두 선생님과 함께 강당에 갔습니다. 끝!

en: That's right, Boris. You heard it. Auditorium!
I am so lucky to be with the smartest boy in class.
ko: 반에서 제일 똑똑한 남자랑 같이 있다니 나 진짜 운 좋다.

en: Some words are so thug.
Thug? Oh, remember I told you the G-H sometimes sounds like an F.
ko: 터그? 아, G-H가 가끔 F 소리가 난다고 했던 거 기억나지.

en: It's tough, like rough, or laugh.
Oh, Joey, make love to me tonight.
ko: 오, 조이, 오늘 밤 나랑 사랑을 나눠줘.

en: That is good English.
Okay, I'd like everybody to take out their homework on the subjunctive tense.
Joe, they didn't do it? I got it.
ko: 조, 걔네 안 했어? 나는 했어.

en: If the present tense of the verb to be is I am,
ko: 동사 to be의 현재형이 I am이면,

en: then the subjunctive tense is if I...
Joey? If I was. Oh, I'm sorry. That's not correct.
ko: 조이? If I was. 아, 미안해요. 그건 틀렸어요.

en: It's if I were.
Very good, Boris.
Joey, you're stupid. Joey is stupid, Renata.
ko: 조이, 넌 바보야. 조이는 바보예요, 레나타.
